channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2025 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2025 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Operando X-ray imaging reveals size-dependent evolution of cobalt oxide thermochemical material during thermal redox cycles
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 17 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-66174-0

Using operando synchrotron X-ray imaging, the study reveals 3D morphological and chemical changes of thermochemical particles during redox cycles, uncovering degradation mechanisms and insights for improved thermal energy materials.
item.dc:title: Operando X-ray imaging reveals size-dependent evolution of cobalt oxide thermochemical material during thermal redox cycles
item.dc:creator: Yuxiang Peng
Lyu Zhou
Madeline Van Winkle
Cheng-Chu Chung
Youyang Zhao
John Mangum
Xianghui Xiao
Katherine Jungjohann
Judith Vidal
Shuang Cui
Mingyuan Ge
Yu-chen Karen Chen-Wiegart

item.title: Spatial correlation at the boson peak frequency in amorphous materials
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 17 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67574-y

Photonic time crystals (PTCs) have unveiled unusual band structures and phenomena due to temporal modulation of optical properties. Here, the authors address non-Hermitian features of PTCs within a purely Hermitian Hamiltonian description, bridging classical and quantum approaches.
item.dc:title: Spatial correlation at the boson peak frequency in amorphous materials
item.dc:creator: X. Y. Li
H. P. Zhang
S. Lan
D. L. Abernathy
C. H. Hu
L. R. Fan
M. Z. Li
X.-L. Wang
